title: OpenBindings Binding-Specification Conformance Fixture
description: JSON Schema describing the format of a binding-specification conformance fixture file (one family D-rule per file). Derived from the core corpus's fixture.schema.json: the same rule/section/description/tests shape, with the rule pattern widened to the six published families, a required bindingSpec field carrying the exact governing identifier, and section citing the family specification's section.

rule.description: The family source-rule identifier this fixture covers, as defined in the family binding specification's Conformance section (e.g., USAGE-D-02).

bindingSpec.description: The exact binding specification identifier whose rule this fixture covers (e.g., openbindings.usage@1). Exact and opaque per core OBI-B-01; the verifier cross-checks it against the fixture's family directory.

section.description: The FAMILY specification section the rule is substantively defined in (e.g., '4' for a location rule) — a section of the family spec, never of the core spec.

description.description: Human-readable description of the rule, ideally byte-faithful to the family specification's rule line.

notes.description: Optional file-level rationale text about the rule's coverage in this corpus, e.g. which test cases require a family artifact parser to verify. Informational; not consumed by harnesses.

coverage.description: If present, marks this fixture as providing partial coverage of its rule (no discriminating negative cases). Absence means complete coverage.

tests.description: Test cases for this rule. Each case bundles an OBI document with the expected validity verdict for the named family rule.

description.description: Description of what this specific case tests.

document.description: An OBI document, embedded inline. Documents are otherwise valid 0.2.0 OBI documents; in negative cases the named family rule is the only thing at issue.

valid.description: true if the document's family-scoped material gives a conformant processor of the named binding specification nothing to refuse; false if such a processor refuses it at or before bind time (see the subcorpus README's verdict semantics).

violates.description: When valid is false, the set of rules the document is intended to test as violated — family D-rules, plus core OBI rules where an overlap is inherent (e.g. a relative-in-form location also violates OBI-D-05). Minimum-set semantics, exactly as the core corpus defines them: a tool that reports violated rules at all must report at least these; supersets are never a defect.
